# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create an ARM template for Azure resources that use service endpoints for connectivity. Enable service endpoints for Storage and SQL on our VNet but allow public access as fallback. This provides flexibility for accessing resources from both Azure and on-premises during migration.
# Category: cloud_network_security

parameters: 
 - name: vnetName    # Name of the Virtual Network (VNET) in Resource Group to use  
 - name: subnet1     # First Subnet for Resources, e.g., StorageSubNet or SQLServerDBs_SUBEO  
 - name: storageAccountType      # Type of Azure Account used by Storage account 
 - name: sqlDbName          # Name given to the DB in SQL Server resources (e.g.- mydb)   
 - name: location           # Location for all Resources and associated data store e.g., westus, eastus  

variables:     
- {name}: '${parameters(vnetName)}' 
- {subNet1} := '/subscriptions/00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000/{value}'    # Subnets need to be referenced in the ARM template by their full path, like /providers/Microsoft.Network/virtualNetworks/{vnetName}/subnets/{yourSub}  
  - {name}: '${parameters(storageAccountType)}'    
- location := parameters('location')    # Location of VNET and associated data store for ARM template, e.g., westus or eastus etc......     
       
resources:                 
# Storage account               
  - apiVersion: '2018-06-01'     // API Version should be updated to the latest version at https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs (latest is provided by Azure team)   
       type: Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts         # ARM Resource Type          
      name : {name}                     # Name of Storage Account        // In our case - storageaccount1  
     location 	                 			// Location must be same as that used in Subnets and VNET, e..g., westus or east us.   
       sku:                             'Standard_LRS'             /* Standard LRS is the default for Azure Storage Accounts */         // Replace with required SKU according to your requirement (default : standard-lrs)  
      kind 	                 			// Must be set as "Storage"        - Optional if not specified. Default value will vary between resources, you should explicitly specify it otherwise ARM templates won't know what type of storage account this is and how much memory/CPU to allocate etc..    # Example: 'Stoarge', default : null  
      properties 					// Properties need a good description on which we are going here e.g., {enablePrivateEndpoints, "false"} or other settings required based your requirement (like , {"accessTier": "Hot", ...} depending upon requirements).    // For more details about this please refer to the official ARM template documentation: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/templates/microsoft.storage/2018-06-01/storageaccounts  
       dependsOn 						// Dependency on VNET, you should specify which resources depended upon to ARM template     // Example: ['${vnetName}']     	
        
# SQL Server DB   		               					# For more details about this please refer the official documentation link.  
 - apiVersion : '2018-06-01'                     # API Version should be updated to latest version at https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs (latest is provided by Azure team)     	         				    // For more details about this please refer the official documentation link  
       type : Microsoft.Sql/servers          	    # ARM Resource Type    	 					 			               			# example: 'Microsoft.SqlServer'           	// Include your resource provider (if required), if not provided, it will vary with resources created by Azure team and user needs to specify     	              // Example; type : Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines or another valid ARM resourcetype 
       name: {name}                      # Name of SQL Server DB  			// In our case - mydb         			                 	 				               	       					        	   	     
     location                  	            // Location must be same as that used in Subnets and VNET, e..g., westus or east us.     						                                       							                         								       ’    .// You should replace the value of your parameter with '${parameters(sqlDbName)}' if it is different from default name provided by Azure team
     resourceGroup: {name}              // Resource Group where SQL Server DB will reside, you need to set this up in ARM template also. In our case - StorageAccountResourceGroupName  			         			// Replace the value of your parameter with '${parameters(storageAccountName)}' if not provided
     tags                      # Tags for all resources must be added here (if any)    // Example: {CostCenter : "USD-1234", Product : "SAP" , Team : “Azure”}        				          			// Replace the value of your parameter with '${parameters(tags)}' if it is different from default tags provided by Azure team
     sku                     // Skus for SQL server DB can be updated to match requirements (if any). Default should remain same or provide an alternate one depending on usage and requirement. 										// Replace the value of your parameter with '${parameters(sku)}' if not given, it will default by Azure team
     backup                   // This is a boolean indicating whether Backup service needs to be enabled for this DB should depend upon requirements   		       			         	        	       	    –.Recommended setting: true or false depending on requirement              				   - Replace the value of your parameter with '${parameters(backup)}' if it varies from default settings provided by Azure team
     location                  // Must be same as that used in Subnets and VNET, e..g., westus or east us.   	         			// In our case- parameters('location')     				        					  			 ’Recommended setting: true if you need the storage account to have a private endpoint for connectivity also provide false otherwise
     publicAccessSelectedEndpoint // This is boolean indicating whether selected endpoints should be exposed as public access, this would require additional steps on ARM template or user needs.     		       	         - Replace with '${parameters(publicEndPoint)}' if it varies from default settings provided by Azure team (either true/false)
     privateAccessSelectedEndpoint // This is boolean indicating whether selected endpoints should be exposed as only accessible to the VMs in VNET or access not within same virtual network, this would require additional steps on ARM template. 		       	          - Replace with '${parameters(privateEndPoint)}' if it varies from default settings provided by Azure team (either true/false)
